title: Listen and Choose a Response — Requests and Offers

questions.stem: The syllabus says the paper should be double-spaced. Does that include the bibliography too?

options.A: Yes, the entire document should be double-spaced.
options.C: The syllabus was updated last month.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for specific formatting clarification, so a direct yes/no answer with explanation is most helpful.

questions.stem: Would you like some more pizza? There's still half a pie left.

options.A: No thanks, I'm full. It was delicious though!
options.C: I ate lunch two hours ago.

questions.explanation: The speaker is offering food, so politely declining with a compliment is courteous.

questions.stem: Could you hold on for just a minute? I'm almost done with this email.

options.C: I sent three emails this morning.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking someone to wait briefly, so a patient and accommodating response is appropriate.

questions.stem: I know the group wants to meet at 8 AM, but I think that's too early for a weekend.

options.A: I see your point. How about 10 AM instead?
options.C: I wake up at 6 AM every day.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing disagreement with a proposed time, so acknowledging their concern and offering a compromise is constructive.

questions.stem: I didn't catch what you said about the assignment deadline. Could you repeat that?

options.A: Of course. The deadline is next Tuesday at midnight.
options.B: You should pay more attention in class.

questions.explanation: The speaker missed important information and is asking for it to be repeated, so patiently providing the information again is helpful.

questions.stem: I can't believe we're going to present at the national conference! This is huge!

options.A: I know! We've worked so hard for this moment.
options.C: The national conference is in Chicago this year.

questions.explanation: The speaker is expressing excitement about shared success, so matching their enthusiasm and acknowledging the achievement is the natural response.

questions.stem: How do I apply for a parking permit for next semester?

options.A: You can apply online through the transportation portal starting next week.
options.C: The parking lot is always full.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking how to complete a procedure, so providing clear step-by-step guidance is most helpful.

questions.stem: I accidentally deleted the shared folder. I feel terrible about it.

options.A: Don't panic. IT can probably restore it from backup.
options.B: You should be more careful with shared files.
options.C: I never use that folder anyway.

questions.explanation: The speaker is upset about making a mistake, so a calming response with a practical solution is most supportive.

audioSegments.text: The syllabus says the paper should be double-spaced. Does that include the bibliography too?
Would you like some more pizza? There's still half a pie left.
Could you hold on for just a minute? I'm almost done with this email.
I know the group wants to meet at 8 AM, but I think that's too early for a weekend.
I didn't catch what you said about the assignment deadline. Could you repeat that?
I can't believe we're going to present at the national conference! This is huge!
